degraded: F0 scoped to shared/tests with -m 'not slow and not cross_plugin' (3284 passed, 12 skipped, 0 failed). The change is confined to two standalone installer shell scripts + their test file (test_installer_shell_scripts.py, 16/16) with no import coupling to other modules, so the shared suite is a superset of the affected surface.

test_installer_shell_scripts.py grew to 367 LOC (>300 LOC guideline). It is NOT in shipwright_bloat_baseline.json, so the anti-ratchet pre-commit gate does not block; it is a single coherent installer-shell regression suite (F33-F39). The Group H detective audit surfaces the crossing post-merge.

behaviors.behavior: update-marketplace.sh python resolver: a python3 found by `command -v` but failing `--version` (Microsoft Store stub, exit 49) is skipped, and the next working candidate (python) is selected without aborting under set -euo pipefail
verify-setup.sh python resolver: same stub-skip behavior via the consolidated single SW_PYTHON resolver (reused by both the prerequisite check and the .env.local parser)
POSIX no-regression: a host with only a working python3 (no `python`) still resolves python3 — the added `--version` guard does not break the F37 case
End-to-end: the full marketplace cache sync completes on this Windows box instead of aborting at exit 49 right after 'Full sync:'
behaviors.evidence: manual e2e: `bash scripts/update-marketplace.sh` -> '=== Done. 13 synced, 0 skipped, 0 errors ===', exit 0; plus F0.5 CLI surface_verification tests_run=16 exit_code=0
